description: This figure is a comparative logarithmic plot displaying precipitation strengthening magnitude versus precipitation fraction for carbides in microalloyed and Fe-Mn-Al-C steels. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 500 MPa, while the horizontal axis spans precipitation fraction from 0.0001 to 1 on a logarithmic scale. Four phases are compared as vertical range bars: NbC spans roughly 70–180 MPa at fractions near 0.0003–0.001; VC spans approximately 0–125 MPa near 0.001; TiC exhibits the largest range of about 120–450 MPa around 0.001–0.003; and κ-carbide covers roughly 100–350 MPa at higher fractions visually near 0.1–0.3. Accompanying text reports that the κ-phase strengthening potential is 100–350 MPa for a volume fraction of approximately 0.05–0.35. The chart illustrates that κ-carbides in austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels deliver substantial precipitation strengthening comparable to TiC, but require significantly higher volume fractions, highlighting their distinctive age-hardening behavior in low-density steels.
